title: Interactions and Actions
description: Learn how to interact with web elements using Playwright actions

questions.question: Which method is used to type text into an input field?
questions.explanation: page.fill() is the recommended method to type text into input fields in Playwright.

questions.question: Complete the code to click a button:
questions.explanation: The click() method is used to click on elements in Playwright.

questions.question: Playwright automatically scrolls elements into view before interacting with them.
questions.explanation: Playwright automatically scrolls elements into view as part of its actionability checks.

questions.question: Which method performs a double-click on an element?
questions.explanation: Both dblclick() and click({ clickCount: 2 }) can be used to double-click an element.

questions.question: Complete the code to select an option from a dropdown:
questions.explanation: selectOption() is used to select options from dropdown menus.

questions.question: The check() method can be used on both checkboxes and radio buttons.
questions.explanation: The check() method works for both checkboxes and radio buttons in Playwright.

questions.question: What is the difference between fill() and type()?
options: fill() is faster as it sets the value directly
type() simulates keyboard events character by character
questions.explanation: fill() is faster, clears the field first, and sets value directly. type() simulates actual keyboard events.

questions.question: What's wrong with this code?
questions.explanation: Actions cannot be chained this way. Each action should be performed on the appropriate element separately.

questions.question: Complete the code to hover over an element:
questions.explanation: The hover() method is used to move the mouse over an element.

questions.question: Which method uploads a file to an input element?
questions.explanation: setInputFiles() is used to upload files to file input elements in Playwright.
